reviews.review_text:
I put down the controller as the credits rolled, and watched them until the end. For the first time ever. Listening to the incredible music and thinking about the unbelievable game and emotional story I just experienced. The twists and turns will make you reconsider what evil is, and whether your best intentions can lead down that path.
 
Such a great story and lore, enhanced by the fantastic performances of Ben Starr and Anthony Howell. Not to mention the Budapest philharmonic orchestra that is sometimes on par with Expedition 33's soundtrack.
The sound effects are amongst the most satisfying you will ever hear, I can still hear them in my head days after completing it. While visual style and art direction are simply superb, even the doubters will grow to love every bit of it. 

Combat is simply the best of any game to date. 

Before I continue to combat, I'd like to address a complaint I had seen in some reviews that actually put me on the fence when deciding to play the game. Some people said the bosses are damage-sponges and have way too much health. I'm here to tell you that this is simply not true. 

The game has 2 important mechanics among others. Brink parrying and reflecting. Reflection is basically hitting the enemy's hand while it's about to hit you.  When done successfully, you will instantly eliminate up to half of the boss's stamina, and put him in a staggered state. This allows you to inflict a lot of damage, and once the stamina bar is depleted, he goes into an exhausted state and you can go crazy with some epic combos and take out 25-30% of their health.  What I'm assuming a lot of people do is simply try to dodge and evade all hits, which would of course take FOREVER to bring down a boss. If you use parrying and reflection boss fights will take 2-3min on average which is pretty standard. 

That being said, the combat is unprecedented and perhaps the best ever made. You have 3 different weapons you can use, each with a very large skill tree of its own, plus one tree in common. Making every weapon unique in terms of actions and gameplay. On top of which you have dozens upon dozens of upgrades, augments, dozens of armor sets that each give you specific strengths and abilities, some of which you can combine with others. 
Hundreds of various items you can find to enhance your character and upgrade your weapons until they become unstoppable.. This game is so incredibly rich, and the moves you end up making with your chosen weapon make you feel like a god.  It is so clean, and beautiful to play, that every encounter is one you will look forward to instead of dreading like in many other such games. 

Important: Unlike every other game in the genre, you NEVER feel like you're wasting time. Even if a boss takes you many tries, it's never time wasted because *you get loads of XP with every try*. Every try you do gives you the opportunity to upgrade your character before the next try, you're ALWAYS progressing. This should be a gold standard and changes everything. 

It's an Action RPG game, It's actually heavy on RPG from the gear side, sets and stats, and is not difficult even on Expert difficulty, which is what I played. I 100% the achievements in about 58hrs and played some NG+ after that, and I'm an average 50 year old gamer. 
There are normal and a recently added easy difficulty mode for those who prefer less of a challenge. On top of which you can summon a ghost helper to fight bosses, and upgrade them until they are better than you are. 
I cannot speak to that part much as I did not even summon it once, but it's there.

The closest games to this one that I've played are Jedi survivor and Lies of P, with a tad bit of Sekiro perhaps. I adored all 3 of those games, and Khazan just surpassed them all for me. If you enjoyed any of those games, Khazan is for you. 

I never wanted a sequel this bad of all my games and I own many. The sad reality is that the ARC Raiders CEO took over Nexon and decided the team is better off making Live Service games cause there is more money in those. Had they gone forward with Khazan as a trilogy this game would've exploded as many in this genre do after a few entries. It is just the richest and most perfect game of its type. Hopefully they come back to it when they see how many of us adored the first. 

My dear friends, Khazan The First Berserker is an unforgettable experience that is nearly perfect on every front, and with the best combat you will ever play.  

100% Recommended.

Soulslike with one of the most satisfying combat I’ve played. The combat and progression system is actually closer to an action RPG than a soulslike, similar to Wukong.

The story is serviceable, interesting enough to keep things moving along. It's a revenge story, but I feel like it doesn't hit as much as it should because we barely get to spend any time with General Khazan before his downfall. A prologue game exploring that period woulc actually be really interesting, though I've heard the studio or team behind the game has been dissolved? Such a shame.

The bosses are easily one of the best parts of the game. Their designs are really cool, and they fully showcase the combat system, forcing you to utilize the full range of tools in your arsenal (especially if you play in the hardest difficulty). I've only done 1 playthrough with the greatsword and it is VERY satisfying to use.

The area design is solid, and by soulslike standards the blade nexus (bonfire) placement and shortcuts are well done. I never really felt confused or frustrated while traversing the map. One thing I sometimes struggle with in these kinds of level designs is figuring out which areas are optional and which paths are meant for progression. I like exploring every single nook and cranny before progressing and I think the game does a good job distinguishing between the two.

There are also the usual annoying mini platforming sections with hazardous subtances where the slightest fall sends you back to the blade nexus but overall it's still enjoyable

One other thing I really appreciate is the drip. The armor and weapon designs are peak, especially the unique/boss gear, I'm not sure what the proper term is, basically equipment that require boss drops to craft. I really enjoy dressing up Khazan with all these cool armor they provided.

One of the better souls like out there. The combat is absolutely fantastic!

It’s easy to pick up, flashy, responsive and most importantly super fun. On the other hand, the story is serviceable and is nothing to write home about. For years, I’ve looked for Sekiro adjacent experiences and this game definitely scratches that itch. If you’re thinking of picking this up because you’re a sucker for souls games, then look no further coz Khazan’s got your back.

It’s a real same what happened to the devs tho, I would have loved to see what they made next…
Amazing game. Satisfying combat and environment visually very attractive

Very good combat, enjoyed alot, the bosses are by far the best part, mobbing and running around the levels  can feel very tedious and boring, same mobs over and over again. Especially bad are the "side missions". Basically the same level again but sometimes nightime or you start in a different corner and the boss is different but otherwise the same.

Again, the bosses are so fire... and the combat feels so smooth, I would still recommend it if you have run out of FromSoft games to play
Hell upon Nexon for shutting the studio down, game was great and I wish we got more.
Great game had fun playing it wasn't super hard but really fun Also looks great really love the atmosphere of it.

RIP To the greatest Souls like game and hands down BEST gameplay i have ever experienced in any of the souls games, Good parry windows, Amazing skill tree system. So sad there will not be anymore content for this game. I will never forget the experience 10/10

This is an awesome souls-like game that I wish more people knew about and played. In the beginning there were some hiccups, some quality of life issues, but they managed to get those fixed. 

The story is okay, the level design is okay, the dialogue I really don't like, but the graphics actually pretty good and the game runs pretty well. There isn't any blurry or noisy textures or artifacts. 

The combat and boss encounters is where this game really shines. I want more bosses, I want more armor sets, but I can't because the developers weren't able to meet their mark, whatever that was, and have been reallocated to a different project. So we may never get additional content for the game like DLC and other side content.

I recommend this game if you like souls-like games like Sekiro and Lies of P.

It's a good soulslike and I'm having fun with it. 

But the automatic stamina regeneration of bosses even when I'm dealing damage to them sometimes makes me wanna throw my controller at the screen.

I would definitely not recommend this to someone if this sort of gameplay mechanic is a deal breaker to them. If it's not, then be sure not to miss out on this fantastic experience and enjoy the game!

Has ruined my standards for combat in games, damn near perfect gameplay.
Runs great, sounds great, plays great!

A game that went far too under the radar imo. The story and level design are just about the only major drawbacks, and both are easily passable in terms of quality.

A souls like that isn't afraid of reaching out of its own niche bubble, offering multiple builds, combos, upgrades amongst 3 weapon choices. Enough freedom to play as you wish while constricting gameplay to allow proper balance. It plays like Nioh where aggression is key and the pressure must stay on.

Graphics are alright, performance is good, and character models/npc designs surprised me at how well they are made. 

The story is dull, many characters just exist for no reason, do nothing, or die for almost no reason at all if not rather shallow reasons. It follows the same formula of a "broken world" that all souls likes follow. I think this game could have benefited from doing something different, maybe have things take place as the world is breaking or have an option to save towns, villages, or cities. With the opportunity to rescue them or lose them; having rescued them would likely bring allies to fight with you and other rewards+story options.

Level design is also very linear with rather unrewarding exploration, but it works very well as a hack and slash so it doesn't drag the game down too much.

Boss design is the highlight imo. Only about 2 that I can think of are bad, and they're for very obvious reasons; being out of place with the way the combat and game design is.

With some improvements and change in game direction I can see this being a promising franchise. 

A small game worth the price with plenty of fun to be had.

Finishing this game in my top games of all time. Super fun. 

Difficult, yes, but it never felt unfair or impossible. Story was okay and level design and enemy variety was getting a bit tedious by the end, but ultimately this is a boss rush game. It sounds intimidating, but the quality of life features made it very fun. This is one of the few games I've played where you get to play a powerful character vs a powerful boss and feel balanced. Highly recommend to those who enjoy challenging boss fights and great action. Even if you're not necessarily good at these games, again, the QoL features really compensates for the difficulty.
Pre-ordered the game and definitely enjoyed playing it... however the big bow on level 4-1 doesnt properly apply physics so its gonna have to be a 0/10 for me

Khazan much fun. After learning how to use all three weapons, ultimate challenge still hard as hell. 
Wish there was more to the game.
Decent souls-like if you're looking to kill some time and have fun. Linear game, not very difficult compared to other games in the genre, but worth a play through.

This game is Fantastic and deserves lots of praise. The visuals, combat and Boss designs are fantastic. the story isn't it's strongest aspect but is nothing to scoff at either. progression is fun and even allows you to toss the bosses around sometimes without sacrificing the difficulty. 

Overall I highly reccomend this game if you enjoy Souls-like games but want a fresh take on the genre.
Game is good but there is a bug that locks you out of a side mission, which you need to get the true ending.

AMAZING boss fights with music and EXCELLENT combat + visuals throughout. Decent story with very linear progression, along with decent level design. Played it on the hardest difficulty and loved it a lot. Will 100% recommend this game.

Honestly, a really good Souls game. As this is my first Souls game, I had high expectations as I watched my friends play Elden Ring, Dark Souls, Black Myth: Wukong, and Sekiro. This game did not disappoint. The graphic is 10, as it has an anime style, which I really like, and the combo is just art. The storyline is easy to follow and understand. Combat, I love it, and I like how the 3 main weapon types are some people may see as a downside for me; it's nice. The bosses are also really good, as in, for beginners, as it doesn't instantly throw you in the deep end, but there is a steep learning curve for beginners. For people looking for another Souls game and grind achievements, I highly recommend it.
10/10 fighting game, still hate both of the witches, only Ozma is a real mage cause he can throw hands

Not sure why this game isn’t super popular. Great game, amazing combat, hard bosses that you actually have to learn rather than spam against, amazing art style, etc. I love the amount of skills you can get for each weapon. Makes for amazing long clean combos. Sure the story could be told a bit better in my opinion, but I think this game is great overall.

This game was my Best Action game of 2025. Phenomenal and crisp combat playstyle, the combat really shines on other things, I didn't like the level design and recycled levels. Recommended 100% but buy it at a sale.

This game was so good, so extremely fun that after finishing it, I returned to Sekiro to once again experience a fighting system so refined and boss fights so epic.
It’s a shame the game didn’t meet sales expectations, and the team that developed this wonder has been reassigned, because it truly deserved more recognition.
So I can only say: if you enjoyed Sekiro, do yourself a favor—buy this game and enjoy it.
The First Berserker: Khazan is an absolute masterpiece . If u like games such as Nioh, Sekiro, etc u should definitely try this one. I really enjoyed this game and definitely I would rate it +9.

I’m sad that we won’t be seeing a sequel as this was a very exceptional foundation for a promising new IP within the “souls-like” genre. 10/10 loved every minute of it and a lot of replay value here. Only issues is the constant red filter over the game and the lack of cutscenes for Female Khazan.

- Fun and challenging combination of GoW and Dark souls
- very stable and optimized
- replayability due diffrent weapon set choices

Sooo much fun! I am not expert by any means and have played less than 10 games in my life! haha but I have learnt a lot with this game. The world is so cool and dark, I have no idea what is going on with the story yet and I have been killed by the bosses over 50 times each! But I am enjoying this game so much! I would totally recommend it for you all experts =) The only think I dislike about it, is that the I am not sure yet how to use the loot and stuff, I find it so confusing =( lol
Great battle system, amazing bosses and cutscenes, overall very smooth experience.

Enough of a challenge to keep me engaged but never truly punishing especially when you figure out what fighting style works for you (Hyper Aggressive). Just a fun game for me and that's all I care about.

Amazing, it is unfortunate that NEXOM disbanded this group of developers. Khazan has the best souls-like bosses out of all the games. Master Piece in combat, hope to see this level of combat and boss design again.

This is a Souls game with the most difficult boss fight mechanics. Lies of Peril can't even compare to this game in terms of boss fight difficulty at its highest level.
I thought Arlechino was the hardest boss I could face until I encountered Maluca, Hismar, and Ozma.

And I'm very satisfied with the boss fights in this game.
I hope there's a sequel.

Khazan is kinda what I imagine Austin looks like.

I found this game to be unexpectedly exceptional and for me had ALMOST no bad bosses. I think that if all the souls games had similar combat systems I would enjoy them a lot more.
This is one of the best souls-like i have ever play. The gameplay is crisp, the story is solid, and the Devs are the best. I can not give this more credit, great job devs!
great and challenging game

Insanely satisfying and compelling combat in this game. Every boss I have faced has felt extremely smooth and gratifying. There are so many combos you can do with the weapon skills and the fact that you can always remap your skill tree makes it very friendly to try new skills. Overall if you love parry focused combat systems then this game is for you.
P.S. If you loved lies of p's combat than you will surely love this game as I believe this game has a slightly better combat system because of the attacks that have to be parried instead of perfect guarded (parry timings are very tight but give a large opening)

This is the best Soulslike for me in decades..ill explain why nioh 2 was a good soulslike but its overwhelming with two many stuff to learn and skill tree is complex..this game took inspiration from that and made the skill tree 1000 times better ,combat is the peakest in the starting it might appear slow but aroung the mid game boss espescially maluca the game turns into hack and slash ,the only reason people glaze lies of p over this is because khazan didnt get cracked so many people didnt get to witness this peak and im extremely devasted that the studio got shut down because of funding . was waiting for KHAZAN 2 ..U Bring any soulslike to debate id prove u why khazan is better ..best optimization since its madee in unreal engine 4 .the only part ill be honest with myself is exploration could be made a bit better, for me the exploration was 8 /10 but the game overall was 100/10 
full acheivements on steam and platinum on ps5 .

This is one of the best games I've played recently.

I hadn't known I liked souls-like games till Elden Ring, so my experience with them is not huge. The First Berserker is not an open-world, still I enjoyed playing it a lot. Really liked visuals, they chose anime-ish style rather than photo-realism, and this is the very right decision, from my perspective. Three different play styles are nice, I finished normal difficulty with the spear, but thinking about starting a new game with dual wielding. Audio and music are amazing, especially the soundtrack for the fight with Reese. That's my favorite.

I heard the game didn't meet expectations from financial point of view - I'm absolutely no ideas why. The game is great. No bugs. No lags or hiccups (although I'm playing on 4080 laptop, different graphics cards may have issues). I really hope the team behind the game will not be laid off and will release more games like this.

It has a good story, good gameplay, and good challenges. The game allows you to be as agile as you want, depending on the weapon you choose, and you can change your playstyle throughout the campaign.

The formula of this game is very similar to that of Nioh, where you choose a mission or side mission and enter that world that guides you to the end where you must defeat a boss. The enemies become repetitive from a certain point in the game and some bosses of these side missions can repeat some of the main missions but stronger and with more advanced attack sets.

As its tags indicate, this is a Souls-like Action RPG. It has a good variety of enemies and bosses, and each one challenges you in a different way, requiring you to use your skills, resources, and ingenuity to defeat them. Each enemy has its own unfair aspects, but that only adds to the challenge.

The weapon and armor crafting and upgrade system is simple, and it also allows you to craft entire sets to obtain all the passive abilities they provide (once you find their recipe). I think that the more you progress, the more value the attribute percentages granted by these passives should have, since each set has its own, and these percentages don't change when you level up or get better loot of the same type, except for the amount of armor they provide or the random attributes they generate (which are separate from the passives).

Phantom Form is a useful ability if you want to tank an ability you're not used to or when you're about to defeat a boss so it doesn't kill you. Personally, for a special transformation, it doesn't offer much beyond tanking a few hits. It's very slow, and the attacks and abilities don't do much more than I could do without activating it. They could get a lot more out of it, but I feel it doesn't offer much in the way of increased damage or mobility.

I really enjoyed the game, its graphics, animations, and atmosphere; the music was a big plus too. I would highly recommend playing it if you really like these kinds of games, but it might be a bit of a learning curve for those new to the genre.

Excelent game, fun to play, too many Stylish movesets, diferent bosses require diferent strategies, very very good videogame if you love souls like games 
10/10

Finally finished the game. Combat wise 9/10 , Story wise 3/10, atmosphere 10/10,
If you play it for the story i am sorry to disappoint its not there. 
I kid you not the unskippable credits are longer then the entire story cutscenes. If you fear to change to the female version of Khazan, just do it you wont miss anything (cause there are no cutscenes for her).
maybe 50% into the game you kinda feel the story builds up and leads some where, but afterwards i feel like Ai could make up a better direction.
that said combat is super fun, the outfits are really cool, enemy's and Boss fight's are challenging, 
I felt disappointed that Phantoms story goes no where (***spoilers***->) literally at the end just he vanished and you cannot pick up what happend to him, just to be a 1 liner at the literal end "Chaos is still there and so am i"? Okay...? i guess? besides that its a fun game to pass time, its just not that deep as i hoped.

The combat feels smooth and the blocking is great!  The art in this game is incredible! The easy mode is a great way to get used to the mechanics of the game and boss rotations.  Normal is very challenging, especially late game.  I highly recommend this game!

This game right here was the start of my loves for souls and soulslike games. I first play this through a friends Steam deck since I didn't have good pc then and absolutely loving it despite getting my bum handed to me. The combat is incredibly smooth with the ways you can chain together skills and various basic attacks, much more of an fast paced action RPG than the typical souls game. You dictate the flow of combat, you are the berserker, fight like one.
Story is a little bit weak, nothing amazing but it's pretty good regardless. 8/10

10/10 because i cant find anything i dislike so far

edit: 100% at 35%, still 10/10
- the story has no twisted or insane depth but I got no problem with that, the narrative is easy to follow and understand.
- Bosses got moderate difficulty, I think it did a good job balancing hard and fun.
The game is great and looks amazing. Just don't pay extra for the art & music. it is NOT a download, it just gives you access to music and art through the game menu.
great game with great animations and fighting. skill tree is awesome as well i would highly recommend this game to anybody interested

A few minor nitpicks aside, this is one of the best soulslikes/souls hybrid games I've ever played. The combat in particular stands out, but the art design, boss roster, and normal enemies are all worthy of praise in their own right. I also really love a few smaller things this game does like limit how much healing you can have. I believe 8 heals is the max and I missed one so I finished the game with only 7 heals. That feels way more balanced that the 15 or so healing flasks you end up with in most From Software games, as an example.

As for those nitpicks, I do think status effects are used a bit too much (especially when there are multiple in a single fight), the level design is kinda meh, the story is pretty underwhelming, and I think the game is a little too long for its own good. Almost all bosses get reused for side missions and altho it took me a total of about 45 hours to beat, I had seen basically every regular enemy by hour 15-20 and they were all just cycled in and out from there onwards. The enemies are mostly well designed and fun, but I think I would have liked the game more if it were about 30% shorter with less reuse of enemies and bosses.

Overall, I think if you value and love high skill fast paced souls-ish combat from other games like Lies of P, Sekiro, and Stellar Blade, I think you'll really love Khazan.
Wow, this is an amazing game. The graphics is good, the action is good, it's above and beyond my expectations.

I have mixed feelings on this game. The combat is absolutely incredible, so incredibly satisfying to learn and play. Most of the bosses are fun, the areas are fun. The skill tree is awesome with all the combos you can do, I only played great sword and it was awesome. The progression is the worst thing I think, the first part of the game was the most fun, when you were a noob and had little skills the game was very challenging. Blade phantom, Viper, Volbaino, Maluca, Elamain, Skalpel (best boss in the game). Viper took me like 20 tries, the rest about 5. These bosses were amazing and were a challenge. All incredible bosses and challenging for the stage of the game you are at. But after that the game was so incredibly underwhelming. Princess Ilyna is anti fun just a worse version of Bellerian. Hismar was a joke of a boss, had like 5 moves that took 3 business days to hit you, killed 2nd try. Reese attacks barely did anything nor was challenging, killed 1st try. Ozma true ending barely did anything his attacks didn't hurt nor was hard to guard or dodge, killed 2nd try. I was level 144 on expert difficulty without summons. I think the problem here is that these bosses would have been hard earlier on with the level you and how far you are through the skill tree. At this point of the game you have so many skills and all these buffs that the bosses should have scaled with you, but they didn't.

The other systems in the game is very cool tho, I love the transmog system, its perfect. The bonus challenges for enemies are fun. The training area is awesome, never used it but I could see how it would be good for some people to try out combos. Love that you can refight bosses for more loot and rare material for the armor sets, its fun to go back and get into the groove and farm them.

This game desperately needs a DLC, like in souls fashion the DLC needs to be extremely challenging. As a soulslike its a very easy one, then again I could just be very good at them considering I've played all Fromsoft souls, lords of the fallen, Lies of P, and Black Myth Wukong. The 2 boss rush modes after completing the game suck ass too, they are just artificial difficulty. Nothing fun about it. 7/10

boring game, expected more really and i shouldn't have from a soulslike. game has nothing good for it except for the parries and art style, but level design starts to get mid after a certain point with enemy placement and environment. 5/10

I initially liked this game a lot. As a fan of both Nioh and Darksouls, this seems like a middle ground. Yet, out of all the soulslikes game this is one I cannot recommend.  However, the reason is for replayability. Bosses, combat and level design are solid, fun an enjoyable. But replaying and honing your build is pointless.

My main issue is on NG+, actually. NG+ takes all your equipment and gear and takes it to the start of the game. You know, the basics.. You are put in the level 140 area with your level 145 gear. Cool. However, enemies had 0 change outside stat scaling.  So enemies, which where balanced on the player having the bare bones to fight, are still balanced on the player having barebones are roflstomped out of existence. So you think, ok, I will change difficulty. So from easy you change to Normal. And if you started the game on normal you can change to... easy? You can change the difficulty from normal to easy, even after finishing the game. what...the...hell...

Ok, well actually, maybe I'm exaggerating. Actually, you DO unlock a new difficulty, Hardcore. Doesn´t change enemy placement or moves, it also boosts enemy stats and restricts the user interface and limits some mechanics   Ok, fine, at least its not just a stat boost. Huh, weird, I can´t change the difficulty to hardcore...Can you guess what they did? You can ONLY play on Hardcore on a fresh, NEW GAME. NOT NG+!!! 

This means you got two options: 1) Play again on ng+ mindnumbingly, you further polish your build and gear that already roflstomps everything or 2)Start from zero on a higher difficulty, effectively being a tempo loss AND arrive at the SAME issue once you get to end of the game!

Both choices are just unfun. I don't know why they did not allow to get into Hardcore in NG+ on your first playthrough.

Go play Nioh instead if you want that combat, gear farming, build making cocaine.

THE best soulslike out there, quality on the level of Fromsoftware themselves. story isn't most groundbreaking, but its more than made up for by the stunning art direction and the ludicrously fun combat. 

♥♥♥♥ NEXON for axing Neople btw. a crying shame we won't see more from this studio because they made something insanely special
This was amazing. This was some of the best combat I have played in a long time. Just good fun gaming!

combat is pretty good, but prior to playing this game people acted as if this was the best thing since sliced bread, in reality, it is not.

Enemy variety is non existent
side quests (I've done all of them so far) are so boring
the gameplay look is going through a bunch of boring areas and fighting a boss at the end
buy the game if on sale.

So just screw the players that's been keeping it alive and our higher level just keep giving handouts that are level 25 could at least make it where we could craft it people are keeping your game alive and you couldn't reward us you guys are shady
Dark Souls + Darksiders + God of War 3 =  First Berserker Khazan.  If you like those games, chances are you will like this one too.
Great, satisfying combat. Good story. Definitely recommend!
I dont want to play anymore. I cried. I cried so much. She did nothing wrong. She didnt deserve that.
I hate nioh and this game is the nioh in the worst ever form

Most fun souls-like to me, very action oriented and they have a system wherein you dont ever get locked out of progression just cause you cant beat a boss. Every try on a boss gives you baseline lacrima(exp) so try enough times you'll have enough to keep leveling regardless.
The Jarlings alone are worth buying the game. 11/10
really fun and difficult
it's fun when u start to know how to play the game 8/10
best game ive played in a really long time
Game is awesome, as hard in some parts as Sekiro. Highly recommended.
favorite combat system in a soulslike game
great souls-like though the side missions are very repetitive
some bosses were pretty difficult
last boss fight was great
Best combat I've ever seen in a soulslike.
Best combat of all soulslikes
It is a good challenge
great game
Game good
fun ahh game
better than elden ring
good
just fun
its like sekiro and i love sekiro
awesome and like super cool and awesome
absolutely a must play
nice
great
Absolutely Awesome!
larp larp larp
Absolute Cinema
cooooooooooooooool
GOOD
bekar hai
khazan
